I do investment.”\n[L52] \n[L53] He stared at me, and this time there was something like understanding. Not full understanding. The first bud of it. The embryo, if you like, tiny and alive.\n[L54] \n[L55] Mr. Greeves wandered past. He glanced at our work, eyebrows lifting. “Good diagram,” he said, sounding surprised despite himself. “Nice and clean.”\n[L56] \n[L57] Charlie’s hand tightened on the pencil, but he didn’t look up. He didn’t bask. He just kept working, as if praise was a thing that might vanish if he moved too fast.\n# 【13†Chap02.md†file_00000000629c72089b44450d8f19c0ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file13\nMclick Target: \"6:13\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] I turned my head just enough for the whisperer to know I’d heard. I didn’t even have to identify her. “Because I like getting full marks,” I said, pleasantly. “And I like working with people who don’t waste time showing off.”\n[L2] \n[L3] Silence. A delicious, tidy silence.\n[L4] \n[L5] Mr. Greeves recovered, puzzlement slowly fading from his face. “Alright then. Celeste and Charlie. Good.”\n[L6] \n[L7] I walked my chair over without asking permission from the air. Charlie stared at me as if I’d sat down inside his head.\n[L8] \n[L9] “You don’t have to do this...” he began timidly. I could read a powerful emotion in his voice and in his eyes: *it's the girl from the loo*! \n[L10] \n[L11] “I know.”\n[L12] \n[L13] He blinked and swallowed hard. That lone word seemed to unsettle him more than reassurance would have. I placed my notebook on the desk between us and looked at the question set.\n[L14] \n[L15] “Pick one.”\n[L16] \n[L17] “I—” he began, and faltered. The page might as well have been written in smoke.\n[L18] \n[L19] I watched him slowly collect himself and set himself to the task. He didn’t panic theatrically. He didn’t joke. He didn’t make excuses. He scanned. Not the way someone scans for answers, but the way someone scans for structure.\n[L20] \n[L21] My eyes dropped to his own notebook as he moved it — careful, almost fussy, aligning the corners of the paper with the desk edge. I noticed his handwriting: neat, consistent, slanted slightly right, as if it had manners.\n[L22] \n[L23] “You’re good at geometry, aren't you?”\n[L24] \n[L25] His head came up sharply. “What?”\n[L26] \n[L27] “Geometry. You're good at it. Your diagrams: very precise.”\n[L28] \n[L29] He looked genuinely confused, as if the possibility of being observed outside his failures had never occurred to him. “I’m... okay, I guess.”\n[L30] \n[L31] “You’re better than okay.” I tapped the question set. “Pick one with a diagram. A shape. Something that lives in space, not in a string of symbols.”\n[L32] \n[L33] He hesitated, then pointed with his pencil. “This one. The triangle... with the angle bisector.”\n[L34] \n[L35] “Good.” I nodded. “You do the diagram. Make it clean. Label it properly. I’ll do the algebraic part and write the explanation. Then you check me for logic. Deal?”\n[L36] \n[L37] He stared at me. “You... trust me to check you?”\n[L38] \n[L39] That was the real question. Not about maths. About hierarchy.\n[L40] \n[L41] “I trust your eyes,” I replied. “They’re honest.”\n[L42] \n[L43] His ears went faintly pink. Not in a flattered way — more like embarrassment at being assigned a virtue.\n[L44] \n[L45] He bent over the page. His pencil moved and the triangle appeared with a crispness that felt almost calming. Clean lines. Honest angles.\n[L46] \n[L47] While he worked, I listened to the classroom. The buzz of other pairs. The smugness of boys who’d paired up for safety, girls who’d paired up for comfort. I heard my name used in little asides, the way people taste words to see if they’re sweet.\n[L48] \n[L49] Charlie drew his angle bisector and then paused, frowning.\n[L50] \n[L51] “What?”\n[L52] \n[L53] He pointed. “If you call that angle *x*... then this one has to be *x* too, because of the bisector. But the problem statement says this angle is thirty degrees, which means *x* is fifteen. Which means... your ratio is fixed.”\n[L54] \n[L55] He said it softly, as if he expected to be corrected.\n# 【14†Chap02.md†file_00000000629c72089b44450d8f19c0ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file14\nMclick Target: \"6:14\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] # School Corridor, Late Afternoon\n[L2] \n[L3] By the time Mr. Greeves started writing *GROUP TASK* on the board, the room had already made its decision.\n[L4] \n[L5] It wasn’t an official decision, not one you could point to in a rulebook, but it lived in the way chairs angled away from Charlie, in the little coughs people used to cover their discomfort, in the speed at which everyone suddenly found the floor fascinating. I watched it happen with the same detached interest I’d watch a flock of birds turn as one body: instinct, cowardice, and the lazy relief of belonging.\n[L6] \n[L7] Mr. Greeves tapped the chalk like it owed him money. “Alright. You’ve all had your practice test. You’ve all had your feelings about your practice test. Today, you’re going to make something sensible out of it.”\n[L8] \n[L9] A few kids chuckled. Not because it was funny — because it was safe.\n[L10] \n[L11] “Pairs,” he said, underlining it twice. “Pick a question from the set. Solve it. Then produce a one-page explanation that someone *else* could follow. If you can’t explain it, you can’t do it.”\n[L12] \n[L13] There was the usual scrape of chairs, the low panic of social arrangements. Everyone moved fast, because speed looked like confidence. I didn’t move. I didn’t need to. People came to me.\n[L14] \n[L15] “Celeste, want to—”  \n[L16] “Celeste, I saved you a—”  \n[L17] “Celeste, I already have—”  \n[L18] \n[L19] I gave them my polite face and none of my answer. My attention drifted to the back left, where Charlie sat. The boy of wrong place, wrong door. \n[L20] \n[L21] He wasn’t ugly. He wasn’t particularly strange. He just sat in a way that tried to be smaller than the desk allowed. He was... uncollected. Like a thing people had decided was worthless and therefore never bothered to look at properly. Thin. Shorter than most of the boys, which seemed to bother them more than it bothered him. His uniform shirt sat awkwardly on his frame — too big at the shoulders, too loose at the waist — as if it belonged to someone older and louder.\n[L22] \n[L23] I knew I should have clocked him before, in the loo. I just never noticed him in class. Nobody did. The held-back boy. The one who re-did Year 11 because maths had eaten him alive the first time. People said it with the same tone they used for a failed appliance: *still doesn’t work.*\n[L24] \n[L25] I felt a fleeting twinge of guilt. Just because of not having noticed it was the same lad. Weird.\n[L26] \n[L27] Mr. Greeves said, “If you’re still unpaired in thirty seconds, I’ll pair you.”\n[L28] \n[L29] That was the real threat. Not the task. Not the maths. Being seen as someone who had to be assigned.\n[L30] \n[L31] Charlie’s eyes flicked around the room: quick, skittish, looking down as much as he looked around, not begging, but scanning. When he realised no one was going to volunteer themselves to be his partner, his mouth tightened in a way I recognised. It wasn’t anger. It was resignation borne of experience. He’d already accepted his lot to be humiliated by the teacher as well as rejected by his peers.\n[L32] \n[L33] This almost certainly had happened to him before. In this class. By this teacher and classmates. And I had been there? And not noticed?\n[L34] \n[L35] “Time,” Mr. Greeves said. “Right. Charlie—”\n[L36] \n[L37] “Me,” I said, before he could finish the sentence.\n[L38] \n[L39] The room’s attention snapped to me like elastic. You could feel it in the air—the sudden recalculation. It made me want to smile, but I didn’t. Power is best used as if you hardly notice you have it.\nVisible: 0% - 100%",
  "summary": null,
  "assets": null,
  "tether_id": null
}

      "summary": "Evaluating LoRA integration with GGUF models",
      "content": "I’m diving into how the workflow integrates LoRA with a GGUF model. Looks like the setup uses a model with GGUF unet and applies LoRA during runtime via hooks, instead of separate patching nodes. However, there could be limitations with this approach, given LoRA’s usual reliance on .safetensors.",

daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  1st woman on the right \"moimeme3\", young and shorter, has light skin with professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  She wears a dress with an off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel. She holds herself upright because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains. Her dark brown curls nestle atop her crown, coiled back neatly behind ears trimmed delicately with pearl studs nestled close beside lobes awaiting attention—but none drawn upon now.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene.  2nd woman on the left \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. The dress features softly gathered ruffles at the square neckline and short puffed sleeves edged with fine lace-like frills—a touch of romantic innocence. A central seam runs down from the bustline, adorned with tiny metallic buttons that gleam faintly under the sky’s mercy. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear. She is the taller, buxom woman, wears a gown of muted rose-pink linen, its fabric drape suggesting both grace and subtle weight; it flows like watercolor across her form, catching the ambient light with delicate sheen along creases that whisper movement even when stillness reigns. The dress features softly gathered ruffles at the square neckline and short puffed sleeves edged with fine lace-like frills—a touch of romantic innocence. A central seam runs down from the bustline, adorned with tiny metallic buttons that gleam faintly under the sky’s mercy. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear.","Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene."],"color":"#332922","bgcolor":"#593930"}],"links":[[7124,569,0,571,2,"STRING"],[7127,572,0,570,0,"*"],[7128,573,0,572,0,"INT"],[7131,572,1,576,0,"*"],[7132,572,0,571,0,"IMAGE"]],"groups":[],"definitions":{"subgraphs":[{"id":"7b90b761-3eb6-4fea-a398-96d6a2b5e423","version":1,"state":{"lastGroupId":1,"lastNodeId":601,"lastLinkId":7239,"lastRerouteId":0},"revision":0,"config":{},"name":"Flux.1Dev-LoRA","inputNode":{"id":-10,"bounding":[-2260.966670185349,-2047.3891440815066,141.390625,280]},"outputNode":{"id":-20,"bounding":[702.1702934243768,-2132.4819807975123,120,80]},"inputs":[{"id":"8f948745-f350-4498-aba2-231500f2195a","name":"seed","type":"INT","linkIds":[7133],"pos":[-2139.576045185349,-2027.3891440815066]},{"id":"90821474-425c-4c53-90e3-600060673de0","name":"guidance","type":"FLOAT","linkIds":[7200],"pos":[-2139.576045185349,-2007.3891440815066]},{"id":"b5baa8e8-74a5-4a2b-8ac8-827597a8e8b6","name":"lora_name","type":"COMBO","linkIds":[7203],"label":"LoRA-A","pos":[-2139.576045185349,-1987.3891440815066]},{"id":"a9c07046-9ed0-45c8-92a7-c8bcb739b8a1","name":"strength_model","type":"FLOAT","linkIds":[7204],"label":"LA-strength_model","pos":[-2139.576045185349,-1967.3891440815066]},{"id":"13a7793d-08c2-4c2e-b242-9da54a0f94b0","name":"strength_clip","type":"FLOAT","linkIds":[7205],"label":"LAstrength_clip","pos":[-2139.576045185349,-1947.3891440815066]},{"id":"a09d1af4-d2a5-49aa-bb72-ffffe73ae45b","name":"lora_name_1","type":"COMBO","linkIds":[7206],"label":"LoRA name for S&R":"Display Any (rgthree)","ue_properties":{"widget_ue_connectable":{},"input_ue_unconnectable":{},"version":"7.3"}},"widgets_values":["In a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  1st woman on the right \"moimeme3\", young and shorter, has light skin with professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  She wears a dress with an off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel. She holds herself upright because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains. Her dark brown curls nestle atop her crown, coiled back neatly behind ears trimmed delicately with pearl studs nestled close beside lobes awaiting attention—but none drawn upon now.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene.  2nd woman on the left \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. She is the taller, buxom woman, wears a gown of muted rose-pink linen, its fabric drape suggesting both grace and subtle weight; it flows like watercolor across her form, catching the ambient light with delicate sheen along creases that whisper movement even when stillness reigns. The dress features softly gathered ruffles at the square neckline and short puffed sleeves edged with fine lace-like frills—a touch of romantic innocence. A central seam runs down from the bustline, adorned with tiny metallic buttons that gleam faintly under the sky’s mercy. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear.  In a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene."],"color":"#222","bgcolor":"#000"},{"id":568,"type":"Note","pos":[-1763.9883409192332,-1871.2148347801954],"size":[357.355720850765,1088.0726339522882],"flags":{"collapsed":false},"order":3,"mode":0,"inputs":[],"outputs":[],"properties":{"ue_properties":{"version":"7.0.1","widget_ue_connectable":{}}},"widgets_values":["1st woman \"moimeme3\", young, has light skin, light brown hair in a loose updo, and is wearing a fitted white tank top paired with a pink cardigan and high-waisted red mini skirt that emphasizes her curvy physique and medium-sized breasts. She also wears black ankle-strap heels, giving a sleek, professional vibe to her outfit.  This photograph captures a provocative interaction between two women in a softly lit room.  In the blurred background, there are two other women seated at a table, engaged in conversation and adding depth to the scene. The room has a light ivory hue which contrasts against their darker outfits, and the textures of the wall and floor are smooth, indicative of modern design elements. Overall, the photograph creates a provocative, intimate atmosphere, emphasizing power dynamics through subtle, suggestive details.   2st woman \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. She is dressed in an all-black ensemble comprising of a button-up shirt and slacks that highlight her slender but shapely figure. She is gently resting her left hand on the face of the 1st woman, applying soft pressure with her fingers while gazing intently into her eyes. This act of intimate interaction exudes a sense of dominance and submission.  This photograph captures a provocative interaction between two women in a softly lit room.  In the blurred background, there are two other women seated at a table, engaged in conversation and adding depth to the scene. The room has a light ivory hue which contrasts against their darker outfits, and the textures of the wall and floor are smooth, indicative of modern design elements. Overall, the photograph creates a provocative, intimate atmosphere, emphasizing power dynamics through subtle, suggestive details. \n\n---\n\nIn a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  1st woman on the right \"moimeme3\", young and shorter, has light skin with professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward where wind might have touched them—or perhaps they merely sway due to gravity's tender pull. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel—the golden thread forming patterns reminiscent of vines entwined with blossoms—and one can almost hear their whispers as threads dance between loom and wearer. She holds herself upright yet relaxed, palms resting lightly over her abdomen near the belt buckle made visible only partially because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains despite being worn without ornamentation beyond modest chains holding charms resembling leaves or stars. Her dark brown curls nestle atop her crown, coiled back neatly behind ears trimmed delicately with pearl studs nestled close beside lobes awaiting attention—but none drawn upon now.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene—not quite dusk nor daybreak but somewhere sweetly suspended mid-sunrise.  2nd woman on the left \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. She is the taller, buxom woman, wears a gown of muted rose-pink linen, its fabric drape suggesting both grace and subtle weight; it flows like watercolor across her form, catching the ambient light with delicate sheen along creases that whisper movement even when stillness reigns. The dress features softly gathered ruffles at the square neckline and short puffed sleeves edged with fine lace-like frills—a touch of romantic innocence. A central seam runs down from the bustline, adorned with tiny metallic buttons that gleam faintly under the sky’s mercy. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear, crowned with a single silver headband that catches the light just enough to shimmer against the earthy tones around her.  In a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear.","Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene."],"color":"#332922","bgcolor":"#593930"}],"links":[[7124,569,0,571,2,"STRING"],[7127,572,0,570,0,"*"],[7128,573,0,572,0,"INT"],[7131,572,1,576,0,"*"],[7132,572,0,571,0,"IMAGE"]],"groups":[],"definitions":{"subgraphs":[{"id":"7b90b761-3eb6-4fea-a398-96d6a2b5e423","version":1,"state":{"lastGroupId":1,"lastNodeId":601,"lastLinkId":7239,"lastRerouteId":0},"revision":0,"config":{},"name":"Flux.1Dev-LoRA","inputNode":{"id":-10,"bounding":[-2260.966670185349,-2047.3891440815066,141.390625,280]},"outputNode":{"id":-20,"bounding":[702.1702934243768,-2132.4819807975123,120,80]},"inputs":[{"id":"8f948745-f350-4498-aba2-231500f2195a","name":"seed","type":"INT","linkIds":[7133],"pos":[-2139.576045185349,-2027.3891440815066]},{"id":"90821474-425c-4c53-90e3-600060673de0","name":"guidance","type":"FLOAT","linkIds":[7200],"pos":[-2139.576045185349,-2007.3891440815066]},{"id":"b5baa8e8-74a5-4a2b-8ac8-827597a8e8b6","name":"lora_name","type":"COMBO","linkIds":[7203],"label":"LoRA-A","pos":[-2139.576045185349,-1987.3891440815066]},{"id":"a9c07046-9ed0-45c8-92a7-c8bcb739b8a1","name":"strength_model","type":"FLOAT","linkIds":[7204],"label":"LA-strength_model","pos":[-2139.576045185349,-1967.3891440815066]},{"id":"13a7793d-08c2-4c2e-b242-9da54a0f94b0","name":"strength_clip","type":"FLOAT","linkIds":[7205],"label":"LAstrength_clip","pos":[-2139.576045185349,-1947.3891440815066]},{"id":"a09d1af4-d2a5-49aa-bb72-ffffe73ae45b","name":"lora_name_1","type":"COMBO","linkIds":[7206],"label":"LoRA professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  She wears a dress with an off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel. She holds herself upright because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains. Her dark brown curls nestle atop her crown, coiled back neatly behind ears trimmed delicately with pearl studs nestled close beside lobes awaiting attention—but none drawn upon now."],"color":"#232","bgcolor":"#353"},{"id":599,"type":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","pos":[-2040.5122420038772,-1406.3931046165205],"size":[423.36625996542625,126.78571877499803],"flags":{},"order":25,"mode":0,"inputs":[{"localized_name":"value","name":"value","type":"STRING","widget":{"name":"value"},"link":7222}],"outputs":[{"localized_name":"STRING","name":"STRING","type":"STRING","links":[7231]}],"title":"2nd Woman","properties":{"cnr_id":"comfy-core","ver":"0.3.66","Node name for S&R":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","ue_properties":{"widget_ue_connectable":{},"input_ue_unconnectable":{}}},"widgets_values":["2nd woman on the left \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. name for S&R":"Display Any (rgthree)","ue_properties":{"widget_ue_connectable":{},"input_ue_unconnectable":{},"version":"7.3"}},"widgets_values":["In a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  1st woman on the right \"moimeme3\", young and shorter, has light skin with professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  She wears a dress with an off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel. She holds herself upright because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains. Her dark brown curls nestle atop her crown, coiled back neatly behind ears trimmed delicately with pearl studs nestled close beside lobes awaiting attention—but none drawn upon now.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene.  2nd woman on the left \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. She is the taller, buxom woman, wears a gown of muted rose-pink linen, its fabric drape suggesting both grace and subtle weight; it flows like watercolor across her form, catching the ambient light with delicate sheen along creases that whisper movement even when stillness reigns. The dress features softly gathered ruffles at the square neckline and short puffed sleeves edged with fine lace-like frills—a touch of romantic innocence. A central seam runs down from the bustline, adorned with tiny metallic buttons that gleam faintly under the sky’s mercy. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear.  In a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene."],"color":"#222","bgcolor":"#000"},{"id":568,"type":"Note","pos":[-1763.9883409192332,-1871.2148347801954],"size":[357.355720850765,1088.0726339522882],"flags":{"collapsed":false},"order":3,"mode":0,"inputs":[],"outputs":[],"properties":{"ue_properties":{"version":"7.0.1","widget_ue_connectable":{}}},"widgets_values":["1st woman \"moimeme3\", young, has light skin, light brown hair in a loose updo, and is wearing a fitted white tank top paired with a pink cardigan and high-waisted red mini skirt that emphasizes her curvy physique and medium-sized breasts. She also wears black ankle-strap heels, giving a sleek, professional vibe to her outfit.  This photograph captures a provocative interaction between two women in a softly lit room.  In the blurred background, there are two other women seated at a table, engaged in conversation and adding depth to the scene. The room has a light ivory hue which contrasts against their darker outfits, and the textures of the wall and floor are smooth, indicative of modern design elements. Overall, the photograph creates a provocative, intimate atmosphere, emphasizing power dynamics through subtle, suggestive details.   2st woman \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. She is dressed in an all-black ensemble comprising of a button-up shirt and slacks that highlight her slender but shapely figure. She is gently resting her left hand on the face of the 1st woman, applying soft pressure with her fingers while gazing intently into her eyes. This act of intimate interaction exudes a sense of dominance and submission.  This photograph captures a provocative interaction between two women in a softly lit room.  In the blurred background, there are two other women seated at a table, engaged in conversation and adding depth to the scene. The room has a light ivory hue which contrasts against their darker outfits, and the textures of the wall and floor are smooth, indicative of modern design elements. Overall, the photograph creates a provocative, intimate atmosphere, emphasizing power dynamics through subtle, suggestive details. \n\n---\n\nIn a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.  1st woman on the right \"moimeme3\", young and shorter, has light skin with professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward where wind might have touched them—or perhaps they merely sway due to gravity's tender pull. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel—the golden thread forming patterns reminiscent of vines entwined with blossoms—and one can almost hear their whispers as threads dance between loom and wearer. She holds herself upright yet relaxed, palms resting lightly over her abdomen near the belt buckle made visible only partially because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains despite being worn without ornamentation beyond modest chains holding charms resembling leaves or stars. Her dark brown curls nestle atop her crown, coiled back neatly behind ears trimmed delicately with pearl studs nestled close beside lobes awaiting attention—but none drawn upon now.  Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the scene—not quite dusk nor daybreak but somewhere sweetly suspended mid-sunrise.  2nd woman on the left \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. She is the taller, buxom woman, wears a gown of muted rose-pink linen, its fabric drape suggesting both grace and subtle weight; it flows like watercolor across her form, catching the ambient light with delicate sheen along creases that whisper movement even when stillness reigns. The dress features softly gathered ruffles at the square neckline and short puffed sleeves edged with fine lace-like frills—a touch of romantic innocence. A central seam runs down from the bustline, adorned with tiny metallic buttons that gleam faintly under the sky’s mercy. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear, crowned with a single silver headband that catches the light just enough to shimmer against the earthy tones around her.  In a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity. young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity."],"color":"#232","bgcolor":"#353"},{"id":592,"type":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","pos":[-2040.5122420038772,-1563.615309915505],"size":[423.36625996542625,126.78571877499803],"flags":{},"order":18,"mode":0,"inputs":[{"localized_name":"value","name":"value","type":"STRING","widget":{"name":"value"},"link":7219}],"outputs":[{"localized_name":"STRING","name":"STRING","type":"STRING","links":[7235]}],"title":"1st Woman","properties":{"cnr_id":"comfy-core","ver":"0.3.66","Node name for S&R":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","ue_properties":{"widget_ue_connectable":{},"input_ue_unconnectable":{}}},"widgets_values":["1st woman on the right \"moimeme3\", young and shorter, has light skin with professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  She wears a dress with an off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel. She holds herself upright because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains. Her dark brown curls nestle atop her crown, coiled back neatly behind ears trimmed delicately with pearl studs nestled close beside lobes awaiting attention—but none drawn upon now."],"color":"#232","bgcolor":"#353"},{"id":599,"type":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","pos":[-2040.5122420038772,-1406.3931046165205],"size":[423.36625996542625,126.78571877499803],"flags":{},"order":25,"mode":0,"inputs":[{"localized_name":"value","name":"value","type":"STRING","widget":{"name":"value"},"link":7222}],"outputs":[{"localized_name":"STRING","name":"STRING","type":"STRING","links":[7231]}],"title":"2nd Woman","properties":{"cnr_id":"comfy-core","ver":"0.3.66","Node name for S&R":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","ue_properties":{"widget_ue_connectable":{},"input_ue_unconnectable":{}}},"widgets_values":["2nd woman on the left \"celestesh\", medium skin and long brown hair falling past her shoulders. She is the taller, buxom woman, wears a gown of muted rose-pink linen, its fabric drape suggesting both grace and subtle weight; it flows like watercolor across her form, catching the ambient light with delicate sheen along creases that whisper movement even when stillness reigns. The dress features softly gathered ruffles at the square neckline and short puffed sleeves edged with fine lace-like frills—a touch of romantic innocence. A central seam runs down from the bustline, adorned with tiny metallic buttons that gleam faintly under the sky’s mercy. Her hands are clasped gently before her waist, fingers interlaced, revealing rings—one simple band on each ring finger—that speak not of ostentation but of intimacy. Around her neck rests a slender gold chain bearing a small, ornate pendant shaped like a miniature flower, echoing the floral motifs hinted at nearby blooms. Her hair is styled into a loose yet elegant braid woven through braids above her ear."],"color":"#232","bgcolor":"#353"},{"id":594,"type":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","pos":[-2040.5122420038772,-1248.628882617032],"size":[424.308699117365,173.90767637194074],"flags":{},"order":20,"mode":0,"inputs":[{"localized_name":"value","name":"value","type":"STRING","widget":{"name":"value"},"link":7223}],"outputs":[{"localized_name":"STRING","name":"STRING","type":"STRING","links":[7214,7226]}],"properties":{"cnr_id":"comfy-core","ver":"0.3.66","Node name for S&R":"PrimitiveStringMultiline","ue_properties":{"widget_ue_connectable":{},"input_ue_unconnectable":{}}},"widgets_values":["Behind these figures stretches lush greenery—an unbroken hedge arching gracefully upward beyond eye level, acting as nature’s curtain separating worlds while simultaneously inviting closer inspection within its bounds. To either flank lies manicured grass verging onto gravel paths whose edges blur together seamlessly with moss-stained stone slabs hinting at old-world charm forgotten except here among such beauty. On the far right edge, clusters of wild roses bloom defiantly amidst scattered petals fallen silently since last night’s breeze passed overhead —their crimson hues contrasting beautifully against emerald foliage surrounding them. Further out reaches dense canopy filled with trees stretching high towards ominously dark clouds cloaked in gray haze, lending depth and mystery to the \"8:4\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] \"name\":\"lora_name\",\"type\":\"COMBO\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"lora_name\"},\"link\":null},{\"label\":\"LA-strength_model\",\"name\":\"strength_model\",\"type\":\"FLOAT\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"strength_model\"},\"link\":null},{\"label\":\"LAstrength_clip\",\"name\":\"strength_clip\",\"type\":\"FLOAT\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"strength_clip\"},\"link\":null},{\"label\":\"LoRA B\",\"name\":\"lora_name_1\",\"type\":\"COMBO\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"lora_name_1\"},\"link\":null},{\"label\":\"LBstrength_model\",\"name\":\"strength_model_1\",\"type\":\"FLOAT\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"strength_model_1\"},\"link\":null},{\"label\":\"LBstrength_clip\",\"name\":\"strength_clip_1\",\"type\":\"FLOAT\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"strength_clip_1\"},\"link\":null},{\"label\":\"Setting\",\"name\":\"value_1\",\"type\":\"STRING\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"value_1\"},\"link\":null},{\"name\":\"value\",\"type\":\"STRING\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"value\"},\"link\":null},{\"name\":\"value_2\",\"type\":\"STRING\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"value_2\"},\"link\":null},{\"name\":\"value_3\",\"type\":\"STRING\",\"widget\":{\"name\":\"value_3\"},\"link\":null}],\"outputs\":[{\"label\":\"IMAGE\",\"localized_name\":\"IMAGE\",\"name\":\"IMAGE\",\"type\":\"IMAGE\",\"links\":[7127,7132]},{\"name\":\"STRING\",\"type\":\"STRING\",\"links\":[7131]}],\"properties\":{\"proxyWidgets\":[[\"-1\",\"seed\"],[\"-1\",\"guidance\"],[\"-1\",\"lora_name\"],[\"-1\",\"strength_model\"],[\"-1\",\"strength_clip\"],[\"-1\",\"lora_name_1\"],[\"-1\",\"strength_model_1\"],[\"-1\",\"strength_clip_1\"],[\"-1\",\"value_1\"],[\"-1\",\"value\"],[\"-1\",\"value_2\"],[\"-1\",\"value_3\"]],\"cnr_id\":\"comfy-core\",\"ver\":\"0.3.51\",\"ue_properties\":{\"version\":\"7.0.1\",\"widget_ue_connectable\":{\"text\":true,\"steps\":true,\"sampler_name\":true,\"scheduler\":true,\"width\":true,\"height\":true,\"batch_size\":true}}},\"widgets_values\":[603308628458591,3.9,\"MoiMeme/moimeme3-04.safetensors\",0.95,0.1,\"celestesh-04.safetensors\",0.95,0.1,\"In a hushed, verdant garden bathed in soft, diffused daylight, two young women stand side by side as if caught in a quiet moment of contemplation or whispered conversation—each turned toward the other with gentle curiosity.\",\"1st woman on the right \\\"moimeme3\\\", young and shorter, has light skin with professional makeup on her face including smokey eyeshadow, mascara and eyeliner and blusher, her light brown hair is styled in a loose updo. Her head is tilted towards the camera and her eyebrows are raised as she looks sidelong at the woman on the left.  She wears a dress with an off-the-shoulder bodice with flounced shoulders framing her décolletage, cinched at the waist beneath which cascades a voluminous skirt lined with multiple tiers of ruffled hemming that billow slightly outward. Along this line, intricate embroidery adorns the front panel. She holds herself upright because of how tightly fitted the corsetry remains.\n# 【5†Chap02.md†file_00000000629c72089b44450d8f19c0ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn8file5\nMclick Target: \"8:5\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] “Exactly,” I said. “That’s the spine of it.”\n[L2] \n[L3] He glanced up at me, his hazel eyes quick, searching. “Why are you doing this?”\n[L4] \n[L5] There it was. The suspicion. The defensive little gate he’d built, because people who were kind to him usually wanted something he couldn’t afford. I didn’t lie. I just chose the angle of truth.\n[L6] \n[L7] “Because you’re being tested in the wrong language,” I said. “And I hate waste.”\n[L8] \n[L9] His pencil hovered.\n[L10] \n[L11] “Waste?” he echoed.\n[L12] \n[L13] “Waste of ability,” I clarified. “Waste of time. Waste of people.” I kept my voice calm. “You’re not behind. You’re misallocated.”\n[L14] \n[L15] He swallowed, and for a second his face did something raw, like he’d nearly believed me and it frightened him.\n[L16] \n[L17] I leaned closer, just enough to make my next words private. “Also,” I added, “you’re going to owe me. Not like that. Practical.”\n[L18] \n[L19] His shoulders stiffened. “What do you mean?”\n[L20] \n[L21] “I mean,” I said, “I don’t do charity. I do investment.”\n[L22] \n[L23] He stared at me, and this time there was something like understanding. Not full understanding. The first bud of it. Tiny. Alive.\n[L24] \n[L25] Mr. Greeves wandered past. He glanced at our work, eyebrows lifting. “Good diagram,” he said, sounding surprised despite himself. “Nice and clean.”\n[L26] \n[L27] Charlie’s hand tightened on the pencil, but he didn’t look up. He didn’t bask. He just kept working, as if praise was a thing that might vanish if he moved too fast.\n[L28] \n[L29] When Mr. Greeves walked away, I said, lightly, “See? You exist. People just don’t like admitting it.”\n[L30] \n[L31] Charlie’s mouth twitched. Not quite a smile. The ghost of one.\n[L32] \n[L33] We finished the solution with no drama. I wrote the explanation in clear steps. He checked every transition like a quiet auditor. When we handed it in, Mr. Greeves nodded at me, then at him, as if he’d suddenly remembered Charlie was part of the room.\n[L34] \n[L35] As the bell went, chairs scraped, and the flock of birds turned again. People flowed past us, and I watched Charlie do what he always did: shrink to let them.\n[L36] \n[L37] I slid my notebook into my bag and stood.\n[L38] \n[L39] “Charlie.”\n[L40] \n[L41] He looked up, automatically attentive, as if my voice had become a cue.\n[L42] \n[L43] “I’m doing something after graduation,” I continued, watching his reaction carefully. “A project. A place. It’s... not school.”\n[L44] \n[L45] His eyes flicked away, then back. Hope, hidden under caution.\n[L46] \n[L47] “I need people who can keep their head,” I said. “People who can do detail. People who can be invisible on purpose.”\n[L48] \n[L49] He frowned slightly. “Why me?”\n[L50] \n[L51] I stepped closer, so my answer didn’t have to compete with the corridor noise.\n[L52] \n[L53] “Because you already know how to survive without applause,” I said. “And because if I don’t pick you, someone else will. Someone stupider.”\n[L54] \n[L55] His breath caught—not romantic, not theatrical. Just the shock of being chosen without a joke attached to it.\n[L56] \n[L57] He nodded once, careful, like he didn’t trust his own voice.\n[L58] \n[L59] “Good,” I said. And then, because it mattered, because strategy without warmth is just cruelty, I softened it by a fraction. “I’ll tell you what it is tomorrow.”\n[L60] \n[L61] He watched me walk away as if the hallway had quietly rearranged itself.\n[L62] \n[L63] And it had.\n[L64] \n[L65] Not because I’d saved him.\n[L66] \n[L67] Because I’d placed him.\n# 【6†Chap02.md†file_00000000d0e072078d61836220373bb9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn8file6\nMclick Target: \"8:6\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] “Exactly,” I said. “That’s the spine of it.”\n[L2] \n[L3] He glanced up at me, his hazeel eyes quick, searching. “Why are you doing this?”\n[L4] \n[L5] There it was. The suspicion. The defensive little gate he’d built, because people who were kind to him usually wanted something he couldn’t afford. I didn’t lie. I just chose the angle of truth.\n[L6] \n[L7] “Because you’re being tested in the wrong language,” I said. “And I hate waste.”\n[L8] \n[L9] His pencil hovered.\n[L10] \n[L11] “Waste?” he echoed.\n[L12] \n[L13] “Waste of ability,” I clarified. “Waste of time. Waste of people.” I kept my voice calm. “You’re not behind. You’re misallocated.”\n[L14] \n[L15] He swallowed, and for a second his face did something raw, like he’d nearly believed me and it frightened him.\n[L16] \n[L17] I leaned closer, just enough to make my next words private. “Also,” I added, “you’re going to owe me. Not in a gross way. In a practical way.”\n[L18] \n[L19] His shoulders stiffened. “What do you mean?”\n[L20] \n[L21] “I mean,” I said, “I don’t do charity. I do investment.”\n[L22] \n[L23] He stared at me, and this time there was something like understanding. Not full understanding. The first bud of it. The embryo, if you like, tiny and alive.\n[L24] \n[L25] Mr. Greeves wandered past. He glanced at our work, eyebrows lifting. “Good diagram,” he said, sounding surprised despite himself. “Nice and clean.”\n[L26] \n[L27] Charlie’s hand tightened on the pencil, but he didn’t look up. He didn’t bask. He just kept working, as if praise was a thing that might vanish if he moved too fast.\n[L28] \n[L29] When Mr. Greeves walked away, I said, lightly, “See? You exist. People just don’t like admitting it.”\n[L30] \n[L31] Charlie’s mouth twitched. Not quite a smile. The ghost of one.\n[L32] \n[L33] We finished the solution with no drama. I wrote the explanation in clear steps. He checked every transition like a quiet auditor. When we handed it in, Mr. Greeves nodded at me, then at him, as if he’d suddenly remembered Charlie was part of the room.\n[L34] \n[L35] As the bell went, chairs scraped, and the flock of birds turned again. People flowed past us, and I watched Charlie do what he always did: shrink to let them.\n[L36] \n[L37] I slid my notebook into my bag and stood.\n[L38] \n[L39] “Charlie.”\n[L40] \n[L41] He looked up, automatically attentive, as if my voice had become a cue.\n[L42] \n[L43] “I’m doing something after graduation,” I continued, watching his reaction carefully. “A project. A place. It’s... not school.”\n[L44] \n[L45] His eyes flicked away, then back. Hope, hidden under caution.\n[L46] \n[L47] “I need people who can keep their head,” I said. “People who can do detail. People who can be invisible on purpose.”\n[L48] \n[L49] He frowned slightly. “Why me?”\n[L50] \n[L51] I stepped closer, so my answer didn’t have to compete with the corridor noise.\n[L52] \n[L53] “Because you already know how to survive without applause,” I said. “And because if I don’t pick you, someone else will. Someone stupider.”\n[L54] \n[L55] His breath caught—not romantic, not theatrical. Just the shock of being chosen without a joke attached to it.\n[L56] \n[L57] He nodded once, careful, like he didn’t trust his own voice.\n[L58] \n[L59] “Good,” I said. And then, because it mattered, because strategy without warmth is just cruelty, I softened it by a fraction. “I’ll tell you what it is tomorrow.”\n[L60] \n[L61] He watched me walk away as if the hallway had quietly rearranged itself.\n[L62] \n[L63] And it had.\n[L64] \n[L65] Not because I’d saved him.\n[L66] \n[L67] Because I’d placed him.\n# 【7†Chap02.md†file_00000000d0e072078d61836220373bb9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn8file7\nMclick Target: \"8:7\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] “Exactly,” I said. “That’s the spine of it.”\n[L2] \n[L3] He glanced up at me, his hazeel eyes quick, searching. “Why are you doing this?”\n[L4] \n[L5] There it was. The suspicion. The defensive little gate he’d built, because people who were kind to him usually wanted something he couldn’t afford. I didn’t lie. I just chose the angle of truth.\n[L6] \n[L7] “Because you’re being tested in the wrong language,” I said. “And I hate waste.”\n[L8] \n[L9] His pencil hovered.\n[L10] \n[L11] “Waste?” he echoed.\n[L12] \n[L13] “Waste of ability,” I clarified. “Waste of time. Waste of people.” I kept my voice calm. “You’re not behind. You’re misallocated.”\n[L14] \n[L15] He swallowed, and for a second his face did something raw, like he’d nearly believed me and it frightened him.\n[L16] \n[L17] I leaned closer, just enough to make my next words private. “Also,” I added, “you’re going to owe me. Not in a gross way. In a practical way.”\n[L18] \n[L19] His shoulders stiffened. “What do you mean?”\n[L20] \n[L21] “I mean,” I said, “I don’t do charity. I do investment.”\n[L22] \n[L23] He stared at me, and this time there was something like understanding. Not full understanding. The first bud of it. The embryo, if you like, tiny and alive.\n[L24] \n[L25] Mr. Greeves wandered past. He glanced at our work, eyebrows lifting. “Good diagram,” he said, sounding surprised despite himself. “Nice and clean.”\n[L26] \n[L27] Charlie’s hand tightened on the pencil, but he didn’t look up. He didn’t bask. He just kept working, as if praise was a thing that might vanish if he moved too fast.\n[L28] \n[L29] When Mr. Greeves walked away, I said, lightly, “See? You exist. People just don’t like admitting it.”\n[L30] \n[L31] Charlie’s mouth twitched. Not quite a smile. The ghost of one.\n[L32] \n[L33] We finished the solution with no drama. I wrote the explanation in clear steps. He checked every transition like a quiet auditor. When we handed it in, Mr. Greeves nodded at me, then at him, as if he’d suddenly remembered Charlie was part of the room.\n[L34] \n[L35] As the bell went, chairs scraped, and the flock of birds turned again. People flowed past us, and I watched Charlie do what he always did: shrink to let them.\n[L36] \n[L37] I slid my notebook into my bag and stood.\n[L38] \n[L39] “Charlie.”\n[L40] \n[L41] He looked up, automatically attentive, as if my voice had become a cue.\n[L42] \n[L43] “I’m doing something after graduation,” I continued, watching his reaction carefully. “A project. A place. It’s... not school.”\n[L44] \n[L45] His eyes flicked away, then back. Hope, hidden under caution.\n[L46] \n[L47] “I need people who can keep their head,” I said. “People who can do detail. People who can be invisible on purpose.”\n[L48] \n[L49] He frowned slightly. “Why me?”\n[L50] \n[L51] I stepped closer, so my answer didn’t have to compete with the corridor noise.\n[L52] \n[L53] “Because you already know how to survive without applause,” I said. “And because if I don’t pick you, someone else will. Someone stupider.”\n[L54] \n[L55] His breath caught—not romantic, not theatrical. Just the shock of being chosen without a joke attached to it.\n[L56] \n[L57] He nodded once, careful, like he didn’t trust his own voice.\n[L58] \n[L59] “Good,” I said. And then, because it mattered, because strategy without warmth is just cruelty, I softened it by a fraction. “I’ll tell you what it is tomorrow.”\n[L60] \n[L61] He watched me walk away as if the hallway had quietly rearranged itself.\n[L62] \n[L63] And it had.\n[L64] \n[L65] Not because I’d saved him.\n[L66] \n[L67] Because I’d placed him.\n# 【8†Chap02.md†file_00000000629c72089b44450d8f19c0ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn8file8\nMclick Target: \"8:8\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] “Geometry. You're good at it. Your diagrams: very precise.”\n[L2] \n[L3] He looked genuinely confused, as if the possibility of being observed outside his failures had never occurred to him. “I’m... okay, I guess.”\n[L4] \n[L5] “You’re better than okay.” I tapped the question set. “Pick one with a diagram. A shape. Something that lives in space, not in a string of symbols.”\n[L6] \n[L7] He hesitated, then pointed with his pencil. “This one. The triangle... with the angle bisector.”\n[L8] \n[L9] “Good.” I nodded. “You do the diagram. Make it clean. Label it properly. I’ll do the algebraic part and write the explanation. Then you check me for logic. Deal?”\n[L10] \n[L11] He stared at me. “You... trust me to check you?”\n[L12] \n[L13] That was the real question. Not about maths. About hierarchy.\n[L14] \n[L15] “I trust your eyes,” I replied. “They’re honest.”\n[L16] \n[L17] His ears went faintly pink. Not in a flattered way — more like embarrassment at being assigned a virtue.\n[L18] \n[L19] He bent over the page. His pencil moved and the triangle appeared with a crispness that felt almost calming. Clean lines. Honest angles.\n[L20] \n[L21] While he worked, I listened to the classroom. The buzz of other pairs. The smugness of boys who’d paired up for safety, girls who’d paired up for comfort. I heard my name used in little asides, the way people taste words to see if they’re sweet.\n[L22] \n[L23] Charlie drew his angle bisector and then paused, frowning.\n[L24] \n[L25] “What?”\n[L26] \n[L27] He pointed. “If you call that angle *x*... then this one has to be *x* too, because of the bisector. But the problem statement says this angle is thirty degrees, which means *x* is fifteen. Which means... your ratio is fixed.”\n[L28] \n[L29] He said it softly, as if he expected to be corrected.\n[L30] \n[L31] I looked where he pointed. He was right. The whole thing collapsed into a simple proportion. I felt a small, satisfied click in my chest. Not because he’d solved it. Because of what it meant: he was competent in a way nobody had bothered to find.\n[L32] \n[L33] “Exactly,” I said. “That’s the spine of it.”\n[L34] \n[L35] He glanced up at me, his hazel eyes quick, searching. “Why are you doing this?”\n[L36] \n[L37] There it was. The suspicion. The defensive little gate he’d built, because people who were kind to him usually wanted something he couldn’t afford. I didn’t lie. I just chose the angle of truth.\n[L38] \n[L39] “Because you’re being tested in the wrong language,” I said. “And I hate waste.”\n[L40] \n[L41] His pencil hovered.\n[L42] \n[L43] “Waste?” he echoed.\n[L44] \n[L45] “Waste of ability,” I clarified. “Waste of time. Waste of people.” I kept my voice calm. “You’re not behind. You’re misallocated.”\n[L46] \n[L47] He swallowed, and for a second his face did something raw, like he’d nearly believed me and it frightened him.\n[L48] \n[L49] I leaned closer, just enough to make my next words private. “Also,” I added, “you’re going to owe me. Not like that. Practical.”\n[L50] \n[L51] His shoulders stiffened. “What do you mean?”\n[L52] \n[L53] “I mean,” I said, “I don’t do charity. I do investment.”\n[L54] \n[L55] He stared at me, and this time there was something like understanding. Not full understanding. The first bud of it. Tiny. Alive.\n[L56] \n[L57] Mr. Greeves wandered past. He glanced at our work, eyebrows lifting. “Good diagram,” he said, sounding surprised despite himself. “Nice and clean.”\n[L58] \n[L59] Charlie’s hand tightened on the pencil, but he didn’t look up. He didn’t bask. He just kept working, as if praise was a thing that might vanish if he moved too fast.\n# 【9†Chap02.md†file_00000000629c72089b44450d8f19c0ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn8file9\nMclick Target: \"8:9\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] I knew I should have clocked him before, in the loo. I just never noticed him in class. Nobody did. The held-back boy. The one who re-did Year 11 because maths had eaten him alive the first time. People said it with the same tone they used for a failed appliance: *still doesn’t work.*\n[L2] \n[L3] I felt a fleeting twinge of guilt. Just because of not having noticed it was the same lad. Weird.\n[L4] \n[L5] Mr. Greeves said, “If you’re still unpaired in thirty seconds, I’ll pair you.”\n[L6] \n[L7] That was the real threat. Not the task. Not the maths. Being seen as someone who had to be assigned.\n[L8] \n[L9] Charlie’s eyes flicked around the room: quick, skittish, looking down as much as he looked around, not begging, but scanning. When he realised no one was going to volunteer themselves to be his partner, his mouth tightened in a way I recognised. It wasn’t anger. It was resignation borne of experience. He’d already accepted his lot to be humiliated by the teacher as well as rejected by his peers.\n[L10] \n[L11] This almost certainly had happened to him before. In this class. By this teacher and classmates. And I had been there? And not noticed?\n[L12] \n[L13] “Time,” Mr. Greeves said. “Right. Charlie—”\n[L14] \n[L15] “Me,” I said, before he could finish the sentence.\n[L16] \n[L17] The room’s attention snapped to me like elastic. You could feel it in the air—the sudden recalculation. It made me want to smile, but I didn’t. Power is best used as if you hardly notice you have it.\n[L18] \n[L19] Mr. Greeves blinked. “Celeste?”\n[L20] \n[L21] “I’m with Charlie.” Not: *can I*, not: *would you mind*, but a simple assertion, to affect a course correction of the reality he’d been about to create.\n[L22] \n[L23] A few girls exchanged looks. One boy gave a tiny laugh, like I’d just made a joke he didn’t understand. Someone whispered, not quietly enough, “Why would you do that?”\n[L24] \n[L25] I turned my head just enough for the whisperer to know I’d heard. I didn’t even have to identify her. “Because I like getting full marks,” I said, pleasantly. “And I like working with people who don’t waste time showing off.”\n[L26] \n[L27] Silence. A delicious, tidy silence.\n[L28] \n[L29] Mr. Greeves recovered, puzzlement slowly fading from his face. “Alright then. Celeste and Charlie. Good.”\n[L30] \n[L31] I walked my chair over without asking permission from the air. Charlie stared at me as if I’d sat down inside his head.\n[L32] \n[L33] “You don’t have to do this...” he began timidly. I could read a powerful emotion in his voice and in his eyes: *it's the girl from the loo*! \n[L34] \n[L35] “I know.”\n[L36] \n[L37] He blinked and swallowed hard. That lone word seemed to unsettle him more than reassurance would have. I placed my notebook on the desk between us and looked at the question set.\n[L38] \n[L39] “Pick one.”\n[L40] \n[L41] “I—” he began, and faltered. The page might as well have been written in smoke.\n[L42] \n[L43] I watched him slowly collect himself and set himself to the task. He didn’t panic theatrically. He didn’t joke. He didn’t make excuses. He scanned. Not the way someone scans for answers, but the way someone scans for structure.\n[L44] \n[L45] My eyes dropped to his own notebook as he moved it — careful, almost fussy, aligning the corners of the paper with the desk edge. I noticed his handwriting: neat, consistent, slanted slightly right, as if it had manners.\n[L46] \n[L47] “You’re good at geometry, aren't you?”\n[L48] \n[L49] His head came up sharply. “What?”\n[L50] \n[L51] “Geometry. You're good at it. Your diagrams: very precise.”\n# 【10†Chap02.md†file_00000000d0e072078d61836220373bb9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn8file10\nMclick Target: \"8:10\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] # School Corridor, Late Afternoon\n[L2] \n[L3] By the time Mr. Greeves started writing *GROUP TASK* on the board, the room had already made its decision.\n[L4] \n[L5] It wasn’t an official decision, not one you could point to in a rulebook, but it lived in the way chairs angled away from Charlie, in the little coughs people used to cover their discomfort, in the speed at which everyone suddenly found the floor fascinating. I watched it happen with the same detached interest I’d watch a flock of birds turn as one body: instinct, cowardice, and the lazy relief of belonging.\n[L6] \n[L7] Mr. Greeves tapped the chalk like it owed him money. “Alright. You’ve all had your practice test. You’ve all had your feelings about your practice test. Today, you’re going to make something sensible out of it.”\n[L8] \n[L9] A few kids chuckled. Not because it was funny — because it was safe.\n[L10] \n[L11] “Pairs,” he said, underlining it twice. “Pick a question from the set. Solve it. Then produce a one-page explanation that someone *else* could follow. If you can’t explain it, you can’t do it.”\n[L12] \n[L13] There was the usual scrape of chairs, the low panic of social arrangements. Everyone moved fast, because speed looked like confidence. I didn’t move. I didn’t need to. People came to me.\n[L14] \n[L15] “Celeste, want to—”  \n[L16] “Celeste, I saved you a—”  \n[L17] “Celeste, I already have—”  \n[L18] \n[L19] I gave them my polite face and none of my answer. My attention drifted to the back left, where Charlie sat. The boy of wrong place, wrong door. \n[L20] \n[L21] He wasn’t ugly. He wasn’t even particularly strange even though he sat in a way that tried to be smaller than the desk allowed. He was just... uncollected. Like a thing people had decided was worthless and therefore never bothered to look at properly. Thin. Shorter than most of the boys, which seemed to bother them more than it bothered him. His uniform shirt sat awkwardly on his frame — too big at the shoulders, too loose at the waist — as if it belonged to someone older and louder.\n[L22] \n[L23] I knew I should have placed him before, in the loo. I just never noticed him in class. Nobody did. The held-back boy. The one who re-did Year 11 because maths had eaten him alive the first time. People said it with the same tone they used for a failed appliance: *still doesn’t work.*\n[L24] \n[L25] I felt a fleeting twinge of guilt. Just because of not having noticed it was the same lad. Weird.\n[L26] \n[L27] Mr. Greeves said, “If you’re still unpaired in thirty seconds, I’ll pair you.”\n[L28] \n[L29] That was the real threat. Not the task. Not the maths. Being seen as someone who had to be assigned.\n[L30] \n[L31] Charlie’s eyes flicked around the room: quick, skittish, looking down as much as he looked around, not begging, but scanning. When he realised no one was going to volunteer themselves to be his partner, his mouth tightened in a way I recognised. It wasn’t anger. It was resignation borne of experience. He’d already accepted his lot to be humiliated by the teacher as well as rejected by his peers.\n[L32] \n[L33] This almost certainly had happened to him before. In this class. By this teacher and classmates. And I had been there? And not noticed?\n[L34] \n[L35] “Time,” Mr. Greeves said. “Right. Charlie—”\n[L36] \n[L37] “Me,” I said, before he could finish the sentence.\n[L38] \n[L39] The room’s attention snapped to me like elastic. You could feel it in the air—the sudden recalculation. It made me want to smile, but I didn’t. Power is best used as if you hardly notice you have it.\n# 【11†Chap02.md†file_00000000d0e072078d61836220373bb9】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Chap02.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn8file11\nMclick Target: \"8:11\"\nContent Snippet:\n[L1] # School Corridor, Late Afternoon\n[L2] \n[L3] By the time Mr. Greeves started writing *GROUP TASK* on the board, the room had already made its decision.\n[L4] \n[L5] It wasn’t an official decision, not one you could point to in a rulebook, but it lived in the way chairs angled away from Charlie, in the little coughs people used to cover their discomfort, in the speed at which everyone suddenly found the floor fascinating. I watched it happen with the same detached interest I’d watch a flock of birds turn as one body: instinct, cowardice, and the lazy relief of belonging.\n[L6] \n[L7] Mr. Greeves tapped the chalk like it owed him money. “Alright. You’ve all had your practice test. You’ve all had your feelings about your practice test. Today, you’re going to make something sensible out of it.”\n[L8] \n[L9] A few kids chuckled. I didn’t even have to identify her. “Because I like getting full marks,” I said, pleasantly. “And I like working with people who don’t waste time showing off.”\n[L2] \n[L3] Silence. Good.”\n[L6] \n[L7] I walked my chair over without asking permission from the air. Charlie stared at me as if I’d sat down inside his head.\n[L8] \n[L9] “You don’t have to do this...” he began timidly. I could read a powerful emotion in his voice and in his eyes: *it's the girl from the loo*! \n[L10] \n[L11] “I know.”\n[L12] \n[L13] He blinked and swallowed hard. That lone word seemed to unsettle him more than reassurance would have. I placed my notebook on the desk between us and looked at the question set.\n[L14] \n[L15] “Pick one.”\n[L16] \n[L17] “I—” he began, and faltered. The page might as well have been written in smoke.\n[L18] \n[L19] I watched him slowly collect himself and set himself to the task. He didn’t panic theatrically. He didn’t joke. He didn’t make excuses. He scanned. Not the way someone scans for answers, but the way someone scans for structure.\n[L20] \n[L21] My eyes dropped to his own notebook as he moved it — careful, almost fussy, aligning the corners of the paper with the desk edge. I noticed his handwriting: neat, consistent, slanted slightly right, as if it had manners.\n[L22] \n[L23] “You’re good at geometry, aren't you?”\n[L24] \n[L25] His head came up sharply. “What?”\n[L26] \n[L27] “Geometry. You're good at it. Your diagrams: very precise.”\n[L28] \n[L29] He looked genuinely confused, as if the possibility of being observed outside his failures had never occurred to him. “I’m... okay, I guess.”\n[L30] \n[L31] “You’re better than okay.” I tapped the question set. “Pick one with a diagram. A shape. Something that lives in space, not in a string of symbols.”\n[L32] \n[L33] He hesitated, then pointed with his pencil. “This one. The triangle... with the angle bisector.”\n[L34] \n[L35] “Good.” I nodded. “You do the diagram. Make it clean. Label it properly. I’ll do the algebraic part and write the explanation. Then you check me for logic. Deal?”\n[L36] \n[L37] He stared at me. “You... trust me to check you?”\n[L38] \n[L39] That was the real question. Not about maths. About hierarchy.\n[L40] \n[L41] “I trust your eyes,” I replied. “They’re honest.”\n[L42] \n[L43] His ears went faintly pink. Not in a flattered way — more like embarrassment at being assigned a virtue.\n[L44] \n[L45] He bent over the page. His pencil moved and the triangle appeared with a crispness that felt almost calming. Clean lines. Honest angles.\n[L46] \n[L47] While he worked, I listened to the classroom. The buzz of other pairs. The smugness of boys who’d paired up for safety, girls who’d paired up for comfort. I heard my name used in little asides, the way people taste words to see if they’re sweet.\n[L48] \n[L49] Charlie drew his angle bisector and then paused, frowning.\n[L50] \n[L51] “What?”\n[L52] \n[L53] He pointed. “If you call that angle *x*... then this one has to be *x* too, because of the bisector. But the problem statement says this angle is thirty degrees, which means *x* is fifteen. Which means... your ratio is fixed.”\n[L54] \n[L55] He said it softly, as if he expected to be corrected.\nVisible: 0% - 100%",
  "summary": null,
  "assets": null,
  "tether_id": null
}

    {

## Emily

Ahhh — your “this shouldn’t work… but it *did*” mystery is actually explained by what’s in the workflow you uploaded.

## What’s really happening in your JSON

You currently have **two different SRPO model loaders sitting in the graph**:

1) **`UnetLoaderGGUF`** loading **`gguf/SRPO-Q8_0.gguf`** (collapsed node) fileciteturn9file0L1-L1  
2) **`UNETLoader`** loading **`SRPO/flux.1-dev-SRPO-BFL-bf16.safetensors`** (the “normal” PyTorch/safetensors UNet) fileciteturn9file4L1-L1  

But here’s the punchline:

### Your *actual* generation path is using the **UNETLoader (safetensors)** output, not the GGUF model.
The workflow’s output node is wired to **link 7173**, which comes from **`UNETLoader` (id 443)**. fileciteturn9file4L1-L1  
So the GGUF loader can be present and “look like it’s in use”… while doing absolutely nothing.

That’s why you got a valid image and thought, *“Wait, SRPO works after all?”* — because **the SRPO you used here is the safetensors SRPO**, which behaves like Flux-Dev in all the ways your Hook-LoRA setup expects.

## Why your earlier “SRPO breaks Hook LoRAs” experience can still be true
If your earlier tests were done with **SRPO as GGUF**, then your original hypothesis can still be correct in practice:

- **Hook LoRAs want to patch a Torch UNet** (the safetensors model object).
- **GGUF UNet objects often can’t be patched the same way**, so “model-strength LoRA” either fails, silently does nothing, or causes weird degradation.

And in your graph, your hooks are *very* model-heavy (e.g. `strength_model: 0.95`, `strength_clip: 0.1`). fileciteturn9file2L1-L1  
That’s *exactly* the kind of setup that will look amazing on a safetensors UNet and look “broken” on a GGUF UNet.

## The quick fix: make the graph unambiguous
Do this and you’ll stop chasing ghosts:

### Option A (recommended): stick to safetensors SRPO for Hook LoRAs
- **Delete or disconnect** the `UnetLoaderGGUF` node.
- Keep `UNETLoader → ModelSamplingFlux → SamplerCustomAdvanced` etc.

### Option B: if you *must* use SRPO GGUF
Then assume:
- **model-strength LoRA won’t behave normally**
- You’ll likely need either:
  - a GGUF-compatible LoRA mechanism (if your GGUF node set supports it), **or**
  - run the identity via **CLIP-heavy** prompting/conditioning instead (test with `strength_model = 0`, `strength_clip = 1` as a diagnostic).

## How to confirm what model is *actually* being used (no guessing)
Pick one:

- **Temporarily disconnect** the `UNETLoader` and connect the `UnetLoaderGGUF` into the same model input. If it truly swaps, you’ll see behavior change immediately.
- Or change something that forces recompute (seed, steps), because ComfyUI caching can sometimes make it feel like a swap “worked” when you’re just seeing a cached branch.

## If you paste the exact error text, I’ll tell you precisely which case it is
But even without it: your uploaded workflow already explains the contradiction. **Your successful run wasn’t “SRPO GGUF + hooks”; it was “SRPO safetensors + hooks.”**

If you want, tell me which SRPO you *intended* to test (GGUF Q8 vs BFL bf16 safetensors), and I’ll suggest the cleanest wiring pattern for that choice (including a quick “A/B toggle” so you can flip models without rewiring everything).
